events.summary: Zep board of directors decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives.

evidence_refs.anchor_text: decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives

events.whole_company_scope_note: Termination of Company strategic alternatives process.

exclusions.explanation: August 20, 2013 board discussion concluded it was not an appropriate time to explore strategic alternatives or engage a financial advisor; this is deliberative context rather than a sale-process launch event.

January 7 and January 8, 2014 board meetings concluded it was timely to revisit exploration of strategic alternatives, but the actual process decision is more specifically captured on January 28, 2014.
Distribution of a draft merger agreement without explicit economics is not emitted as a proposal event under the proposal rules.
June 19, 2014 discussion summarized prior bidder concerns and process deterioration already captured from earlier blocks; no distinct new event emitted.

unresolved_mentions: One of the financial buyers contacted during such time was New Mountain Capital.
five of the remaining six interested parties
coverage_notes: Extraction limited to chunk-1 chronology through B069; later events in the merger background are outside the supplied blocks.
No public sale-process announcement or press release appears in the supplied blocks.
April 14, 2014 preliminary indications are only disclosed as an aggregate group with a bid range; individual bidder identities and prices are not provided in the supplied text.
Collective bidder drop events after the May 23, 2014 fire cannot be allocated with certainty to each named bidder from the provided roster.

exclusions.explanation: Statement that no party remained subject to a "don't ask to waive" provision and that the Company received no other inquiries or proposals after the 2014 process is contextual background, not a discrete taxonomy event in this chunk.
Board and management focus on business planning in the second half of 2014 is not a sale-process event under the taxonomy.
Data room access, diligence review, management meetings and site visits are process activities but are not event types in the permitted taxonomy.
Draft merger agreement exchanges, negotiation of legal terms, fairness opinion delivery, and board approval discussions without new explicit bid economics are not separate taxonomy events here.
Go-shop commencement and outreach to 58 parties are not event types available in the permitted taxonomy, and no competing acquisition proposal was submitted.

coverage_notes: Chunk-2 begins after prior-round context stating final_round_inf_ann on March 27, 2014; no new final-round announcement from 2014 is described in these blocks.

Executed transaction economics of $20.05 per share in cash are supported elsewhere in the filing and cross-filing appendix, but the execution event here is grounded in block B113 without separately restating those economics.
No drop event is emitted for other parties because this chunk only references earlier abandonment or withdrawal collectively and not as distinct bidder-specific events.
